accounting.text: Walk me through the three financial statements.
What is the difference between accounts payable and accrued expenses?
What is the difference between cash-basis and accrual accounting?
What is working capital and why does it matter?
Explain the difference between FIFO and LIFO inventory accounting.
What is goodwill and how is it created?
What is the difference between gross profit and operating profit?
What happens to each statement if depreciation increases by $10?
How do you calculate enterprise value?
What is the difference between EBITDA and free cash flow?
A company collects $100 cash from a customer for a service it has not yet performed. Walk me through the three financial statements.
If depreciation increases by $10, what happens to each of the three financial statements? Walk through the full impact assuming a 40% tax rate.
Explain deferred revenue and give an example of when it arises.
How does a stock-based compensation expense flow through the three statements?
What is the difference between capitalizing and expensing a cost? Give an example of each.
Walk me through how a $50 write-down of inventory impacts the three statements.
What is a deferred tax liability and when does it arise?
If a company prepays $100 of rent, walk me through the impact on all three statements.
A company issues $50 of stock to purchase $50 of PP&E. Walk me through the three statements.
How does an inventory write-down affect the three statements?

A company has $100M in revenue and a 10% operating margin. It acquires another company for $50M in cash. The target has $20M in revenue with a 15% margin. Walk me through the combined income statement.
Explain operating leases vs. finance leases under ASC 842 and their impact on the balance sheet.
How does a convertible bond appear on the balance sheet before and after conversion?
Walk me through the accounting treatment for a sale-leaseback transaction.
A company buys back $20M of its own stock. Walk me through the three statements.

valuation.text: What are the main valuation methodologies?
What is the difference between equity value and enterprise value?
Why do we use enterprise value for EV/EBITDA but equity value for P/E?
What is a comparable company analysis and how do you select comps?
Why might one company trade at a higher multiple than a peer?
Walk me through a DCF.
When would you use a DCF vs comps?
How do you calculate WACC?
What drives terminal value?
If you could only use one valuation methodology, which would you choose and why?
What is the difference between trailing and forward multiples? When would you use each?
How do you calculate unlevered free cash flow for a DCF?
What is a football field chart and why is it useful?
Walk me through how you would value a private company.
How does the cost of equity change as a company adds more debt?
What are the advantages and disadvantages of precedent transactions vs comparable companies?
How would you value a company with negative earnings?
Walk me through a DCF step by step. How do you get from revenue to unlevered free cash flow, and how do you discount it back?
How would you value a company with highly cyclical revenue?
What is a sum-of-the-parts valuation and when would you use it?
How do you account for a mid-year convention in a DCF and why does it matter?
Explain the difference between levered and unlevered beta. How do you unlever a beta?
How would you value a pre-revenue biotech company?
What is an LBO valuation floor and how does it differ from a traditional DCF?
A company has two divisions: one growing at 15% and one declining at 5%. How would you approach the valuation?

ma.text: Why would a company want to acquire another company?
What is the difference between a stock deal and a cash deal?
What is a merger vs. an acquisition? What is the practical difference?
What are the typical steps in an M&A process?
Walk me through a basic merger model.
What is accretion/dilution?
What are synergies and how do you estimate them?
What is a fairness opinion and why is it needed?
What is the difference between a horizontal and vertical merger?
How do you determine an appropriate acquisition premium?
What anti-trust concerns might arise in a merger and how are they addressed?
What is a reverse merger and why would a company pursue one?
How do you determine the purchase price in an acquisition?
Walk me through how goodwill is created in an acquisition and what happens if it becomes impaired.
A company with a P/E of 15x acquires a company with a P/E of 10x in an all-stock deal. Is it accretive or dilutive?
Walk me through the purchase price allocation process in detail.
How do earnouts work and when are they used in M&A?
What is a hostile takeover? What defenses can a target company use?
How do you model cost synergies vs. revenue synergies differently?
Explain the difference between asset deals and stock deals from a tax perspective.

lbo.text: How does leverage affect returns in an LBO?
What is a leveraged buyout at a high level?
What types of firms do leveraged buyouts?
Walk me through a basic LBO model.
What makes a good LBO candidate?
How do you calculate returns in an LBO?
What are the key drivers of returns in an LBO?
What is the typical capital structure in an LBO?
What is the difference between senior debt and subordinated debt in an LBO?
How does a dividend recapitalization work?
What are typical debt covenants in an LBO and why do lenders require them?
Walk me through the sources and uses in an LBO.
A PE firm buys a company for $500M using 60% debt. EBITDA is $50M. Walk me through the key assumptions and how you would model returns over a 5-year hold.
How do you sensitize an LBO model? What are the key variables?
What is PIK interest and when is it used in LBOs?
Walk me through a paper LBO for a company with $100M EBITDA, purchased at 8x, with 5x leverage, and a 5-year hold with exit at 8x.
How do management rollover and management equity plans affect LBO returns?
What is a bolt-on acquisition in the context of a PE platform, and how does it affect returns?
Explain the J-curve in private equity. Why does it happen?
How would you evaluate whether a company can support additional leverage?

behavioral.text: Why investment banking?
Walk me through your resume.
What are your strengths?
Why finance and not consulting or tech?
What do you know about our firm?
Tell me about a time you worked on a team.
What's your biggest weakness?
Why should we hire you?
Tell me about a time you failed and what you learned.
Tell me about a time you had to work under a tight deadline. How did you prioritize and deliver?
Where do you see yourself in five years, and how does investment banking fit into that plan?
Tell me about a time you took initiative without being asked.
Describe a situation where you had to analyze a large amount of data to make a decision.
Tell me about a time you received tough feedback. How did you respond?
What is one thing you are doing right now to prepare for a career in banking?
How do you handle stress and long hours?
Tell me about a leadership experience.
Describe a time you had to persuade someone who disagreed with you.
Tell me about an ethical dilemma you faced and how you handled it.
If you had two competing deadlines from two different MDs, how would you handle it?
Tell me about a time you had to make a decision with incomplete information.
Walk me through a deal you followed in the news and explain what interested you about it.
If you were staffed on a deal and realized the analysis had an error after it was sent to the client, what would you do?
What would you do if an associate asked you to do something you thought was wrong?
Tell me about a time you had to manage conflicting priorities from multiple stakeholders.

firm_specific.text: What do you know about our recent deals?
What differentiates {firm_name} from other firms in this space?
Where do you see {firm_name} in five years?
Who are {firm_name}'s main competitors and how do they differ?
What is {firm_name}'s culture like and how would you contribute?
Name a recent transaction {firm_name} advised on and explain why it was significant.
How does {firm_name}'s {division} group compare to the same group at a competitor?
If you joined {firm_name}, what would you want to accomplish in your first year?

market_awareness.text: What's happening in the markets right now?
Tell me about a deal in the news.
Tell me about an IPO or M&A deal from the last six months.
What is the current 10-year Treasury yield and why does it matter?
What sector has been outperforming the S&P 500 recently and why?
What is the Fed doing with interest rates and what is the market expecting?
What is the current state of the M&A market? Is deal volume up or down?
Tell me about a company that recently went public. Was the IPO successful?
Where do you think interest rates are headed?
What sector would you invest in right now and why?
What impact has AI had on the financial services industry?
How has the private credit market evolved and what does it mean for traditional leveraged finance?
What are the biggest risks to global markets right now?
How would a recession impact M&A activity? What types of deals would increase?
Discuss a recent activist investor campaign and whether you agree with the activist's thesis.

brain_teaser.text: How many golf balls can fit in a school bus?
How many piano tuners are there in Chicago?
How many gas stations are there in the United States?
How many tennis balls can fit in this room?
If you had a 5-gallon jug and a 3-gallon jug, how would you measure exactly 4 gallons?
You have 8 balls. One weighs slightly more than the others. Using a balance scale, what is the minimum number of weighings needed to find the heavier ball?
How many windows are there in Manhattan?
How many times a day do a clock's hands overlap?
You are shrunk to the height of a nickel and placed inside a blender. The blades start in 60 seconds. What do you do?
How many people are airborne over the US at any given time?
How would you estimate the weight of the Statue of Liberty?

Two trains are 100 miles apart traveling toward each other at 50 mph each. A fly starts at one train and flies at 75 mph back and forth between them. How far does the fly travel before the trains meet?

You have 100 lockers in a row, all closed. 100 students walk by. The first opens every locker. The second toggles every 2nd locker. The third toggles every 3rd, and so on. After all 100 students, which lockers are open?
If you flip a fair coin until you get two heads in a row, what is the expected number of flips?
You roll two dice. What is the probability that their sum is greater than 7?
There are 25 horses. You can race 5 at a time. What is the minimum number of races to find the top 3 horses?
You have a 3-minute hourglass and a 5-minute hourglass. How do you measure exactly 7 minutes?
How would you calculate the number of degrees between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 3:15?
You have 12 balls and a balance scale. One ball is a different weight (heavier or lighter). How many weighings do you need to find it?
A snail is at the bottom of a 30-foot well. Each day it climbs 3 feet and slides back 2 feet at night. How many days to escape?

market_sizing.text: How big is the US credit card processing market?
Estimate the annual revenue of all Starbucks locations in Manhattan.
How much revenue does the US car wash industry generate annually?
Estimate the number of diapers sold in the US each year.
How big is the US pet food market?
Estimate the total annual revenue of the US fitness industry.
How many iPhones are sold in the US each year?
Estimate the TAM for the US robo-advisory market.
How big is the US student loan market?
Estimate the annual revenue of the global investment banking industry.
How large is the US commercial real estate market by total value?
Estimate the total revenue of all food delivery apps in the US.
How much do Americans spend on coffee per year?
Estimate the total addressable market for electric vehicle charging stations in the US.
How many hotel rooms are there in New York City and what is the average nightly revenue per room?

pitch_a_stock.text: Pitch me a stock you have been following.
If you had $10M to invest in one sector, where would you put it and why?
Pitch me a stock that is currently undervalued by the market.
Pitch me a short. What stock would you bet against and why?
Pitch me a small-cap stock that most people have not heard of.
If you could only own one stock for the next 10 years, what would it be?
Pitch me a stock in the financial services sector.
What is the bull and bear case for a mega-cap tech stock of your choice?
Pitch me an emerging market investment opportunity.
You are an analyst and your PM asks you to find a high-conviction idea in the consumer discretionary space. Walk me through your process and give me a name.
